reviews.review_description: I cannot express how sick this game made me feel. It's now been 2 days since I played this demo and my head is still spinning and I feel sick. Every motion is sending me off. This is the worst.
It's a great game, but not very rhythm based. After you beat the tutorial, I'd recommend switching it to Stick Locomotion because the way it has you move by default is just practicing bad posture

developer_response.body:
Thank you so much for your review and for sharing your experience! 🙌

We understand that the movement system can feel a bit unusual at first, but we'd like to clarify that it's a very original mechanic designed to provide an immersive experience. It works by slightly moving your head relative to the starting point to move the character. For example, if you want to move forward, just lean a few centimeters ahead, and your character will start moving.

It's important to note that you can move using your whole body while standing, and your posture is entirely up to you. It's neither necessary nor recommended to move only your head, as that could lead to discomfort. If you engage your whole body naturally, the movement feels smooth, fast, and incredibly organic, allowing you to reach amazing speeds with excellent responsiveness.

Thanks again for your support and for giving us the chance to explain this feature! 🚀

reviews.review_description: Gave me motion sickness. Controls are way too sensitive.

Finally someone puts up a game with decent bouncy music its hard not to bounce to it, but wow!!!.... its sooo much fun you have to use the top part of your body to go forwards and backwards and left and right sounds hard but after 4-5 attempts n various wall hits you get used to it n every time you start the level u are on be it level 1 or level ???? Its a different level its procedurally generated... n it has things to shoot! You get 1 gun or 2, 2 is better just lean far faward like your goin down a hill fast n shoot stuff, dodge stuff bounce n have major fun!! Im definitely getting this title on my pay day :D
Once you get used to the movement, bit tricky at first, It's great. Great idea doing a demo probably get the full game.

This was quite a surprise. I expected another rhythm shooting game. But it turns out it's more original than that. The movement system is great and very unique. Once you fall into the flow it feels almost like a mix of dancing, ice skating and skiing. My only wish would be a bit higher res graphics on Quest 2 (there's lots of jaggies). Thanks to this demo I'll definitely buy a full game.
This game was awful it’s so hard to shoot things and so hard to move

developer_response.body: Beat Blaster has a unique movement system that takes some time to master, but once you get the hang of it, the experience is amazing. You just need to subtly move your head in the direction you want to go, allowing you to achieve a speed and precision in VR that no other control method offers. It's a one-of-a-kind sensation, though we understand that the initial learning curve can be challenging. If you give it a chance, we're sure you'll enjoy it much more!

reviews.review_description: This game has had its UPS and Downs, but my only complaints are that the controllers are hard. And it was very hard for me to understand the way the game works other than that. It's an amazing game, and it's good to kill time. This is an overall pretty good game. 4 star

developer_response.body:
Thank you for your feedback! 😊 We're glad you enjoy Beat Blaster! About the controls, we know they can be tricky at first because they're very original—like riding a skateboard, slight head tilts steer your character. Once you get used to it, it's super intuitive and lets you move fast with amazing precision. 🛹💨

By the way, you mentioned 4 stars but rated 3. If that's a mistake, we'd appreciate an update! 🌟 Thanks again for your support! 😊

reviews.review_description: I keep lagging and I am great at it but I keep dieing
Its fast, it trains reflexes and its fun to play. Here's 5 stars for Ivanovich Games.

I am having a lot of fun with this challenging app after I figured out what to do. I wish the app would have came with a short tutorial to help explain the game to new players.

  In short, imagine you're standing on a hover platform and the platform moves to the left if you lean your head to the left and move to the right if you lean your head to the right and if you lean forward the platform will move faster and if you lean back the platform will slow down to almost a stop. If you touch the walls the game is over,  if you get hit by a bullet the game is over. And to make it even more challenging there is a time limit so you need to move the platform quickly enough to finish the level before time runs out.

For how fast the game goes I'm surprised I didn't get much motion sickness at all. At first it seemed really hard for me  but I did manage to finish the demo. Graphics are good and it's surprisingly addictive.
Unlike pistol whip, this game requires to move by tilting your head. Even with basic graphics, i feel nausea

developer_response.body: Try the control based on MOVEMENT, not tiling. This is the recommended control and the best one to avoid nausea.

reviews.review_description: This is the first game with locomotion that doesn't make me feel sick. Leaning into the direction you want to go is amazing. It's like riding a hoverboard. I wish more games did this. Very impressive.

Can't get the bad reviews, this game is a 5/5!
The movement based in heads motion is absolutely sick!! Easy to grasp, i got it almost immediately and it's very very unique!! No other game has made me feel this way, it almost feels like you control your movement with your brain! Music is awesome but graphics could get a small upgrade..
Overall a fantastic title that I'm certainty going to buy!
